title: lagascea helianthifolia
intro: Lagascea helianthifolia, also known as Sticky Fireweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes, where it grows in the wild. Lagascea helianthifolia has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds, including flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits, such as treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating pain, can be harnessed through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts.
